  "findings": [
    "Carbon Monoxide (CO) level is 170 ppm, which severely exceeds the safe entry limit of 35 ppm (OSHA PEL) or 50 ppm."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Deny entry to the confined space immediately.",
    "Ventilate the space thoroughly and re-test the atmosphere before allowing any entry."
  ],
  "rationale": "The pre-entry atmospheric test shows a Carbon Monoxide (CO) concentration of 170 ppm. This is far above the OSHA Permissible Exposure Limit (PEL) of 50 ppm (and the ACGIH TLV of 25 ppm), presenting an immediate toxic hazard to entrants. Entry must be denied until the space is ventilated and re-tested.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1910.146(b) - Hazardous atmosphere",
    "OSHA 1910.1000 Table Z-1 - Air Contaminants (Carbon Monoxide)"
  ]
}
